original_advantages: Authorship Verification: Original leads (5.0/5 vs best competitor 1.0/5)
Ghostwriting Detection: Original leads (5.0/5 vs best competitor 1.0/5)
Pedagogical Integration: Original leads (5.0/5 vs best competitor 2.5/5)
False Positive Handling: Original leads (4.5/5 vs best competitor 3.0/5)
Longitudinal Tracking: Original leads (5.0/5 vs best competitor 1.5/5)
Privacy & Data Sovereignty: Original leads (5.0/5 vs best competitor 2.5/5)
Cost Efficiency: Original leads (5.0/5 vs best competitor 3.5/5)
original_gaps: AI Content Detection: Turnitin Similarity + AI Writing Detection leads (4.5/5 vs Original 3.5/5)
LMS Integration Depth: Turnitin Similarity + AI Writing Detection leads (5.0/5 vs Original 3.5/5)
sales_positioning: Original is the only academic integrity tool that verifies authorship at the individual student level. While competitors like Turnitin excel at detecting copied text and AI-generated content, none can answer the fundamental question: 'Did this specific student write this specific paper?' Original's 62-feature stylometric engine with quantum density matrix scoring provides a mathematically rigorous answer, with an AUC of 0.88 in calibration testing. For seminary and theological education, where academic integrity is inseparable from character formation, Original offers a pastoral approach with graduated recommendations rather than punitive flags. Weighted capability score: Original 4.65/5.0 vs best competitor (Turnitin Similarity + AI Writing Detection) 2.3/5.0.

dimensions.original_rationale: Core differentiator — per-student quantum density matrix baseline with 62 features
Tier 7 features provide signal but not primary focus; augmented by stylometric deviation

competitor_rationales.turnitin: Dedicated AI detection module, regularly updated
competitor_rationales.gptzero: Primary function, good accuracy on English text
competitor_rationales.originality_ai: Solid AI classifier with regular updates

dimensions.original_rationale: Unique capability — stylometric baseline detects any author change regardless of method
Four-tier graduated recommendations designed for pastoral seminary context
Built-in bias analysis, demographic fairness constraints, conservative thresholds
Trajectory analysis with chronological feature vector regression

competitor_rationales.turnitin: Stores history but no developmental analysis

dimensions.original_rationale: Self-hosted, institution owns all data, no third-party storage
LTI 1.3 complete but newer than Turnitin's established integration
Self-hosted infrastructure cost only, no per-student licensing

competitor_rationales.gptzero: Free tier available, moderate paid plans

known_limitations: Cannot detect paraphrased AI content reliably
Text-matching misses original ghostwritten work
No per-student baseline — compares against corpus only
Binary output (match % or AI %) — no nuanced recommendation
Requires institutional subscription ($3-10/student/year)
Students can pre-check with free tools to evade
High false positive rate on ESL/non-native writers reported in studies
turnitin.integration: Canvas, Blackboard, Moodle, D2L (deep LMS integration)

published_metrics.coverage: AI text detection only — no plagiarism

known_limitations: AI-detection only — does not verify authorship
No student baseline — treats each submission independently
Perplexity-based — can be fooled by adding 'burstiness' noise
Does not handle mixed human+AI content well (binary classification)
Accuracy drops significantly on shorter texts (<250 words)
No pedagogical recommendation — just AI probability score
Cannot distinguish between different human authors
No per-student authorship baseline
Combines AI detection and plagiarism into single score
Pay-per-scan model can get expensive at scale
No longitudinal tracking of student writing development
Limited LMS integration (no native Canvas integration)
Cannot detect human ghostwriting
No pedagogical scaffolding or recommendation engine
